seoTitle: How to Archive or Restore an Issue in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to archive old Jira issues to declutter your project and how to restore them later if you need the information back.
intro: Old, resolved issues can pile up and clutter your project views over time. This guide covers how to archive an issue to get it out of the way and how to restore it later if you need to reference or reopen it.

steps.heading: Identify issues to archive
steps.body: Look for issues that are fully resolved and no longer need to appear in active searches or boards, such as old completed tickets.

steps.heading: Open the issue you want to archive
steps.body: Navigate to the specific issue you're ready to move out of active view.

steps.heading: Use the archive option
steps.body: Select the archive action from the issue's menu to move it out of standard search and board results.

steps.heading: Confirm the archive
steps.body: Confirm the action, since archiving changes where the issue appears across your project.

steps.heading: Restore an issue when needed
steps.body: If you need the issue back, locate it through the archived issues view and use the restore option to bring it back into active status.

faq.question: Does archiving an issue delete it?
faq.answer: No, archiving hides the issue from normal views and searches, but the data remains and can be restored later.

faq.question: Who can archive issues in Jira?
faq.answer: This typically requires specific permissions, so check with your Jira admin if you don't see the archive option available.

faq.question: Can I search for archived issues?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira provides a separate way to search archived issues, though they won't show up in regular filters or boards by default.

faq.question: Is archiving reversible?
faq.answer: Yes, an archived issue can be restored back to active status at any time using the restore option.
